notes:
Details: water shader crashes the GPU by hitting the thermal throttling on laptops, frame analysis shows signs of lack of LODs, gigantic collision meshes and lack of most of culling techniques. If you wait for around 4 minutes, the game exhausts memory and crashes. Looking at the water or too far into the distance also accellerates this phenomena. The project is also configured to ignore any launch options, engine commands or .ini file changes, which get overritten on startup by hardcoded defaults
Do not buy this game yet, the developer is tone deaf to any feedback regarding performance and the incompetence in handling these kinds of issues is abhorrent. If the situation changes in the future (which I personally hope it does, since artistically it's an awesome game), please do report the workarounds and findings to make the game playable with a discreet performance
title: FUNKYHEART
